payload.source_text:
QUALITY POLICY STATEMENT
Novus Mechanical Services Limited has established a Quality Policy to be consistent with the purpose and context of our organisation.

It provides a framework for the setting and review of objectives in addition to our commitment to satisfy applicable customers’, regulatory and legislative requirements as well as our commitment to continually improve our management system.
Customer focus: As an organisation we have made a commitment to understand our current and future customers’ needs; meet their requirements and strive to exceed their expectations.
Leadership: Our Top Management have committed to creating and maintaining a working environment in which people become fully involved in achieving our objectives.
Engagement of people: As an organisation we recognise that people are the essence of any good business and that their full involvement enables their abilities to be used for our benefit.

Process approach: As an organisation we understand that a desired result is achieved more efficiently when activities and related resources are managed as a process or series of interconnected processes.
Improvement: We have committed to achieving continual improvement across all aspects of our quality management system; it is one of our main annual objectives.
Evidence-based decision making: As an organisation we have committed to only make decisions relating to our QMS following an analysis of relevant data and information.

Relationship management: We recognises that an organisation and the relationship it has with its external providers are interdependent and a mutually beneficial relationship enhances the ability of both to create value.
Our policy is also to meet the requirements of our social, environmental, charitable and legislative responsibilities.

Signed : 							Date: 22/4/2025
           	
Damian Shacklock
Director
NOVUS MECHANICAL SERVICES LTD.

payload.description: Quality objective-setting from policy framework
payload.justification: SG279 defines objective-setting as a strategic function enabled by the quality policy framework.

payload.description: Continual improvement across quality management system
payload.justification: SG284 establishes continual improvement as a core organizational process executed annually as strategic objective.

payload.description: Evidence-based decision making for quality governance
payload.justification: SG285 commits to evidence-based decision making as a strategic governance process.

payload.description: Organizational quality policy as governance artifact
payload.justification: SG278 and SG279 establish quality policy as a foundational governance framework entity, distinct strategic artifact aligned with business purpose and context.

payload.description: Customer needs and expectations as strategic input
payload.justification: SG280 articulates customer needs and expectations as a strategic entity that drives organizational commitment and performance targets.

payload.description: Work environment as enabling organizational context
payload.justification: SG281 identifies work environment as a strategic entity that leadership shapes to enable objective achievement.

payload.description: External provider relationships as strategic partnership construct
payload.justification: SG286 recognizes external provider relationships as distinct strategic entities for value creation.

payload.description: Quality policy alignment with business purpose and context
payload.justification: SG278 establishes the strategic relationship between quality policy and organizational business purpose/context.

payload.description: People engagement linkage to business benefit delivery
payload.justification: SG282 establishes people engagement as a strategic enabler relationship driving business benefit outcomes.

payload.description: Annual continual improvement objective cycle
payload.justification: SG284 establishes continual improvement as a core annual organizational cycle at strategic level.

payload.description: Organizational director as policy authority
payload.justification: SG288 establishes organizational director role as formal policy authorization authority with governance accountability.

payload.description: Leadership responsible for environment creation

payload.description: People as engaged organizational participants

payload.description: External providers as partnership participants

payload.description: Understand and exceed customer expectations
payload.justification: SG280 articulates understanding and exceeding customer expectations as explicit strategic organizational goal.

payload.description: Achieve organizational objectives through enabling environment
payload.justification: SG281 expresses achieving organizational objectives as the goal driving leadership's environment-creation commitment.

payload.description: Create value through external provider partnerships
payload.justification: SG286 identifies value creation as the strategic goal driving external provider relationship management.

payload.description: People engagement as essential organizational value
payload.justification: SG282 establishes people engagement as a core organizational value and strategic principle.

payload.description: Process approach for organizational efficiency
payload.justification: SG283 declares process approach as an organizational efficiency principle guiding strategic operations.

payload.description: Evidence-based decision making as governance foundation
payload.justification: SG285 establishes evidence-based decision making as a foundational principle for quality management governance.

payload.description: Mutually beneficial external relationships as value driver
payload.justification: SG286 establishes mutual benefit as a guiding principle for external provider relationship management.

payload.description: Regulatory compliance commitment requirement
payload.justification: SG279 defines regulatory compliance as a strategic constraint embedded in the quality policy framework purpose.

payload.description: Social responsibility obligation
payload.justification: SG287 declares social responsibility as an organizational obligation constraining strategic action.

payload.description: Environmental responsibility obligation
payload.justification: SG287 declares environmental responsibility as an organizational obligation constraining strategic action.

payload.description: Charitable responsibility obligation
payload.justification: SG287 declares charitable responsibility as an organizational obligation constraining strategic action.

payload.description: Legislative compliance obligation
payload.justification: SG287 declares legislative compliance as an organizational obligation constraining strategic action.

payload.description: Objective-setting for quality policy
payload.justification: SG448 explicitly identifies objective-setting as a process within the policy framework.

payload.description: Customer needs identification and understanding
payload.justification: SG449 describes understanding customer needs as a distinct business process supporting customer focus.

payload.justification: SG449 identifies meeting customer requirements as a process activity distinct from understanding needs.

payload.description: Customer expectation exceeding
payload.justification: SG449 describes exceeding customer expectations as a distinct process beyond merely meeting requirements.

payload.justification: SG453 defines continual improvement as a central business process that operates across all system aspects.

payload.justification: SG454 establishes data analysis as a required process preceding quality management system decisions.

payload.justification: SG455 describes relationship management as a business process that manages interdependent external provider relationships.

payload.description: Quality policy as a foundational organizational document

payload.justification: SG448 explicitly includes regulatory compliance as a policy component, extended by SG456 to include legislative responsibilities.

payload.description: Quality management system
payload.justification: SG453 and SG454 reference the quality management system as a concrete business entity that is the subject of improvement and decision-making processes.

payload.description: External provider relationship
payload.justification: SG455 defines external provider relationships as interdependent business entities that create mutual value.

payload.description: Customer expectation level
payload.justification: SG449 describes customer expectations as a measurable attribute of customer focus requiring understanding and exceeding.

payload.description: People capability assessment
payload.justification: SG451 identifies human capabilities as essential organizational assets, implying they are attributes to be measured and managed.

payload.description: Process interconnection within the quality management system
payload.justification: SG452 establishes interconnected processes as a conceptual relationship model where activities and resources are managed together for efficiency.

payload.description: Customer as recipient of products and services

payload.description: External provider in value creation relationships
payload.justification: SG455 describes external providers as business actors in interdependent relationships with the organization.

payload.description: Leadership role creating enabling environments
payload.justification: SG450 explicitly defines leadership as a conceptual responsibility role for creating environments that enable objective achievement.

payload.description: People engaged in organizational capability deployment
payload.justification: SG451 identifies people engagement as a role concept recognizing human capabilities as essential organizational assets.

payload.description: Align quality policy with organizational purpose and context
payload.justification: SG447 establishes alignment with organizational purpose and context as a foundational goal for quality policy.

payload.description: Meet customer requirements
payload.justification: SG448 and SG449 identify meeting customer requirements as a central quality policy goal.

payload.description: Exceed customer expectations
payload.justification: SG449 articulates exceeding customer expectations as a business goal beyond merely meeting requirements.

payload.description: Achieve organizational objectives through people involvement
payload.justification: SG450 identifies objective achievement through people involvement as a leadership goal.

payload.description: Pursue continual improvement across all quality management system aspects
payload.justification: SG453 explicitly defines continual improvement as a central business objective across all quality management system aspects.

payload.description: Create mutual value through external provider relationships
payload.justification: SG455 identifies mutual value creation as a goal of interdependent external provider relationship management.

payload.description: Fulfill social responsibility commitments
payload.justification: SG456 extends policy scope to include social responsibility as a distinct organizational goal. | SG456 extends policy scope to include environmental responsibility as a distinct organizational goal. | SG456 extends policy scope to include charitable responsibility as a distinct organizational goal.

payload.description: Fulfill legislative responsibility requirements
payload.justification: SG456 extends policy scope to include legislative responsibility as a distinct organizational goal.

payload.description: Customer focus drives business value
payload.justification: SG449 articulates customer focus as a foundational business concept requiring understanding needs, meeting requirements, and exceeding expectations.

payload.description: People capabilities are essential organizational assets
payload.justification: SG451 establishes people engagement as a core principle recognizing human capabilities as essential organizational assets.

payload.description: Process interconnection enhances efficiency
payload.justification: SG452 establishes the process approach principle where activities and resources are managed as interconnected processes for efficiency.

payload.description: Decisions must be evidence-based
payload.justification: SG454 establishes evidence-based decision making as a foundational business principle requiring data analysis before decisions.

payload.description: External relationships are interdependent and mutually beneficial
payload.justification: SG455 defines relationship management as a principle recognizing interdependent external provider relationships that mutually create value.

payload.description: Quality policy must encompass regulatory compliance requirements
payload.justification: SG448 defines regulatory compliance as a mandatory component of the policy framework, constraining policy scope.

payload.description: Quality policy must address continual improvement commitments
payload.justification: SG448 identifies continual improvement commitments as a required element of the policy framework, constraining policy content.

payload.description: Process approach principle requiring activities and resources to be managed as interconnected processes
payload.justification: Signal explicitly identifies a design principle establishing the logical philosophy for process management within the QMS

payload.description: Evidence-based decision making principle requiring decisions to be grounded in data and information analysis
payload.justification: Signal explicitly identifies a design principle establishing the logical requirement for analytical rigor in QMS decision processes

payload.description: Policy document entity with signature block data structure
payload.justification: Signal references a policy document with a signature block, indicating a data entity at the detailed specification level. The signature block is a structural component of the document entity. Confidence matches the Signal's 0.75 as the entity presence is implicit in the signature block description.

payload.description: Temporal validity attribute defining policy document effective date range
payload.justification: Signal explicitly mentions 'temporal validity' as a characteristic of the policy document. At Row 5, this represents a detailed attribute specification capturing time-based validity constraints. Confidence 0.78 reflects clear attribute identification within the signature block context.

payload.description: Organizational authority identifier attribute in signature block
payload.justification: Signal describes 'specific organizational authority' as part of the signature block, indicating a detailed attribute that identifies the authorizing entity. Confidence 0.76 reflects clear attribute presence with slight inference about its implementation as an identifier field.

payload.description: Director role with signature authority for policy document approval
payload.justification: Signal describes a Director signature block indicating organizational authority. At Row 5 (Implementer), this represents the detailed role specification with signature authorization capability. Confidence 0.82 reflects clear role identification though the Signal focuses on the signature mechanism rather than full role definition.

payload.description: Director signature requirement constraint for policy document validity
payload.justification: The presence of a Director signature block implies a validation constraint requiring director-level approval for policy documents. At Row 5, this represents a detailed validation rule. Confidence 0.71 reflects reasonable inference from the signature authority mechanism described.

payload.description: Establishes organizational quality policy aligned with business purpose and context as foundational governance framework.
Defines quality policy framework purpose for objective-setting and commitment to regulatory compliance and continuous improvement.
Articulates strategic commitment to understanding customer needs and exceeding expectations as organizational driver.
Expresses leadership commitment to creating enabling work environment for achieving organizational objectives.
Recognizes people engagement as essential organizational value and strategic enabler for business benefit.
Declares process approach as organizational efficiency principle for achieving desired results.
Establishes continual improvement across quality management as core annual organizational objective.
Commits organization to evidence-based decision making principle for quality management governance.
Recognizes mutually beneficial external provider relationships as strategic driver for value creation.
Declares policy commitment to social, environmental, charitable, and legislative responsibilities as organizational obligations.
Provides formal policy authorization by organizational director establishing governance authority and accountability.
Establishes the conceptual foundation for a quality policy aligned with organizational purpose and context.
Defines the policy framework as encompassing objective-setting, customer requirements, regulatory compliance, and continual improvement commitments.
Articulates customer focus as a business concept requiring understanding of needs, meeting requirements, and exceeding expectations.
Defines leadership as the conceptual responsibility for creating environments that enable objective achievement through people involvement.
Identifies people engagement as a core business concept recognizing human capabilities as essential organizational assets.
Establishes process approach as a conceptual model where activities and resources are managed as interconnected processes for efficiency.
Defines continual improvement as a central business objective across all quality management system aspects.
Establishes evidence-based decision making as a business rule requiring data analysis before quality management system decisions.
Defines relationship management as a conceptual model of interdependent external provider relationships that mutually create value.
Extends policy scope to encompass social, environmental, charitable, and legislative responsibility requirements.
Process approach principle articulating logical management philosophy that activities and resources achieve results through process or interconnected process series.
Evidence-based decision making principle establishing logical requirement that QMS decisions follow data and information analysis.
Director signature block identifying specific organizational authority and temporal validity of policy document.
